data.narrative:
Muon Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $188M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies at same or adjacent stage, including Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, Sierra Space, Varda Space Industries, and Skyryse.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $188M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $357M. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range of $1.1B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue data for the target company.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $304M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $929M. The wide range reflects material dispersion within the peer set—particularly the influence of higher-stage outliers like Sierra Space at $8.0B—and the inherent uncertainty in stage-progression methodology when applied to early-stage venture assets. Stage and specific business model clarity would narrow this band materially.
